summary: This agreement is between National CineMedia and Digital Cinema Destinations Corp, aiming to expand NCM's advertising footprint in markets served by the Network Affiliate. NCM will provide digital content services, while the Network Affiliate will display the content in its theatres. Both parties are obligated to maintain confidentiality and adhere to quality standards. Notable risks include potential infringement and breach of confidentiality, with termination penalties applicable in case of non-compliance. The agreement outlines specific terms and conditions, including revenue sharing and minimum fees, to govern the partnership between the two parties.
confidentiality_clause: “Confidential Information” means any and all technical and non-technical information of or related to either Party, including, without limitation, proprietary information, know-how, the NCM Property and Derived Works, and information related to or regarding either Party’s research and development, finances, suppliers, customers, business forecasts, and marketing plans, in whatever form disclosed or made available. Confidential Information does not include information which: (i) the recipient can demonstrate was already known to it at the time of its receipt hereunder; (ii) is or becomes generally available to the public other than by means of the recipient's breach of its obligations under this Agreement; (iii) is independently obtained from a third party whose disclosure violates no duty of confidentiality; or (iv) is independently developed by or on behalf of the recipient without use of or reliance on any Confidential Information furnished to it under this Agreement.

summary: The purpose of this agreement is for Erchonia Corporation to grant InnerScope Hearing Technologies Inc the exclusive right to distribute low-level laser products. Erchonia must provide products to the distributor, while the distributor must use its best efforts to market and sell the products, meeting minimum performance goals. The distributor is responsible for payment, shipping costs, and taxes. Failure to meet performance goals may result in termination or limitation of exclusivity. The distributor must also maintain records and comply with applicable laws and regulations, and is prohibited from selling competing products without Erchonia's consent.
termination_clause: Either party may terminate this Agreement in the event of a material breach by the other party, provided the breaching party is first given reasonably detailed, written notice of the breach. If the breach is not cured within ten days of such notice, the Agreement will terminate immediately.

summary: This Co-Development Agreement is between PhaseBio Pharmaceuticals Inc. and SFJ Pharmaceuticals X, Ltd. for the development of a pharmaceutical product. PhaseBio will conduct clinical trials and provide operational support, while SFJ will provide financing. Both parties are obligated to comply with applicable laws and regulations. Notable risks include potential termination penalties and liability for non-compliance with anti-corruption laws and regulatory requirements. The agreement also outlines buy-out payments and approval payments, with specific definitions and terms governing the relationship between the parties. Overall, the agreement aims to facilitate the development and approval of the product.

The agreement between Excite@Home and e-centives aims to distribute e-centives' content through the Excite Network and establish co-branded pages. Excite@Home will provide user data, while e-centives will develop and maintain the co-branded application. Both parties will promote each other's services. The agreement involves risks related to user data sharing and potential liabilities. Penalties for termination or breach are not explicitly stated in the provided text, but the parties are expected to comply with the terms to avoid any potential disputes or liabilities. The agreement outlines specific definitions and obligations for both parties to ensure a successful partnership.

This Mobile Application Development Agreement is between VGrab Asia Ltd and a group of private software developers for the development of the Duesey Coffee Chinese Mobile Apps. The developers are responsible for designing and developing the app, while VGrab Asia Ltd will supply graphics, design, and artwork. The agreement has a fixed price of $200,000 and a maximum duration of six months. Payments will be made to the developers upon completion and handover of the project. The agreement includes a bug fixing warranty for three months after final delivery, but any changes to the original specifications will require a written change order.
termination_clause: VAL shall, in the event of Developer committing any breach of any of the terms and conditions of this agreement or for any other reason considered as sufficient, be entitled to terminate this agreement by giving two (2) weeks’ notice in writing and it is applicable only when the project is not completed. If the customer terminates the agreement, then VAL shall compensate the Developer up to the date of termination with a fee calculated on Pro-rata basis. Developer may also terminate this Agreement by giving two (2) weeks’ notice in writing to VAL.

summary: This Development Agreement is between Stryker Orthopaedics and Conformis, Inc. to develop patient-specific instrumentation for Stryker's off-the-shelf knee implant. Conformis must develop the instrumentation according to the R&D program, while Stryker will provide support and funding. The agreement outlines each party's obligations, intellectual property rights, and confidentiality. Notable risks include insolvency events, which could lead to termination, and force majeure events, which may excuse performance. The parties also agree to indemnify each other against certain claims, with liability capped as defined in the agreement.

The Master Joint Development Agreement between iBio Inc. and Beijing CC-Pharming Ltd. aims to establish a long-term working relationship for joint research and development projects related to biopharmaceuticals. iBio grants CC-Pharming a non-exclusive license to use its proprietary technology for manufacturing and developing products in China. Both parties will work together to perform a manufacturing development program and share revenue from product sales. The agreement outlines the roles and responsibilities of each party, including project coordinators and agreement administrators, who will oversee the direction and management of the agreement. Notable risks include the potential for disputes, which will be escalated to designated executives if not resolved, and the limitation of iBio's liability to its minority interest in the collaborative venture.
confidentiality_clause: CC-Pharming hereby expressly acknowledges the validity and highly confidential and proprietary nature of iBio’s Technology and technical information, know-how, documents, materials, software, vectors, constructs, trade secrets, and other valuable business or scientific information that iBio will share with CC-Pharming in order to fulfill the purpose of this Agreement

summary: The agreement between Integrity Incorporated and Time Life, Inc. aims to produce a series of recorded compilations and companion song books featuring praise and worship repertoires, co-branded and co-marketed as "Songs 4 Worship". Integrity will develop the repertoire, obtain master lease and print agreements, and manufacture the products, while Time Life will create artwork, furnish it to Integrity, and pay mastering expenses. The parties will share expenses and marketing efforts, with exclusive distribution rights in certain channels. Time Life guarantees a minimum purchase of 10,000 units per product within 32 months, with potential penalties for non-compliance, although specific liability caps and termination penalties are not explicitly stated in the provided text.

This co-branding agreement is between Skype and TOM Online to create and distribute a customized version of Skype's software in the PRC. The parties agree to grant each other certain rights for co-marketing and co-branding activities. Skype will provide its software and Online BVI will provide its brand features. The agreement outlines definitions for key terms, including adjusted net revenue, affiliate, and confidential information. Each party is responsible for its own expenses and liabilities, with no liability caps or termination penalties explicitly stated in the provided text. The agreement aims to expand the business relationship between the parties.

The joint development agreement between FuelCell Energy, Inc. and ExxonMobil Research and Engineering Company aims to collaborate on research and development projects to evaluate and develop Molten Carbonate Fuel Cells for reducing carbon dioxide emissions. Both parties will work together on mutually agreed upon projects, with ExxonMobil having final decision-making authority on certain matters. FuelCell Energy is restricted from conducting similar work independently or with third parties without ExxonMobil's approval. The agreement establishes a steering committee to oversee the program and technical managers to coordinate technical activities. Non-compliance with the agreement may result in termination, and the parties agree to indemnify each other against certain liabilities.

This agreement is between Bioeq IP AG and Coherus BioSciences, Inc. for the development and commercialization of a biosimilar product. Bioeq grants Coherus an exclusive license for the product in the US, and Coherus must use commercially reasonable efforts to commercialize it. The parties have various obligations, including development, regulatory, and manufacturing responsibilities. The agreement includes provisions for indemnification and limitation of liability, with potential termination penalties if either party fails to meet their obligations. Coherus must also make upfront, milestone, and royalty payments to Bioeq.

The Co-Branding Agreement between VerticalNet and PaperExchange aims to establish a partnership for co-branded career centers and equipment listings. VerticalNet and PaperExchange agree to jointly register URLs and share revenue from the co-branded sites. Each party is obligated to deliver specific content and services, with VerticalNet providing career center and equipment listings content, and PaperExchange providing career and equipment content. The agreement includes provisions for confidentiality and intellectual property protection, with potential termination penalties if either party fails to meet their obligations.
confidentiality_clause: 1.7. Confidential Information shall mean all proprietary and confidential information of a party, including, without limitation, trade secrets, technical information, business information, sales information, customer and potential customer lists and identities, product sales plans, sublicense agreements, inventions, developments, discoveries, software, know-how, methods, techniques, formulae, data, processes and other trade secrets and proprietary ideas, whether or not protectable under patent, trademark, copyright or other areas of law, that the other party has access to or receives, but does not include information that (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of receiving party; (b) was already known to the receiving party at the time it was disclosed to the receiving party, as evidenced by written records of the receiving party; (c) is independently developed by employees of the receiving party who had no knowledge of or access to such information, as evidenced by written records of the receiving party; or (d) is received from a third party who is under no obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party.

Upon termination of this Agreement, (a) each party's liability for any charges, payments or expenses due to the other party that accrued prior to the date of termination shall not be extinguished by termination, and such amounts (if not otherwise due on an earlier date) shall be immediately due and payable on the termination date

summary: This Distribution and Development Agreement is between Sekisui Diagnostics and Qualigen, where Sekisui is appointed as the exclusive distributor of Qualigen's clinical rapid diagnostic test devices. Qualigen is obligated to manufacture and supply the products, while Sekisui will fund the development of future products. The agreement has an exclusivity period until December 31, 2018, and defines various terms such as Adverse Events, Affiliates, and Regulatory Approvals. Notable risks include liability for Adverse Events and Serious Incidents, with the agreement outlining procedures for reporting and addressing such incidents. Termination penalties may apply in case of a Sale Transaction or change of control of Qualigen.

The purpose of this Co-Branding Agreement is for theglobe.com and The Boxlot Company to collaborate on a co-branded service. Theglobe is responsible for delivering page templates and materials, while Boxlot develops and implements the co-branded pages. Boxlot must obtain theglobe's approval before making any changes to the service. Notable risks include termination if Boxlot fails to cure breaches within 12 hours, and theglobe has sole control over the service's look and feel. Boxlot is also responsible for customer support and user agreements, with theglobe having input on user agreements and privacy policies.

The Chase Affiliate Agreement is a contract between Chase Bank USA and an affiliate, outlining the terms and conditions of the Chase Affiliate Program. The purpose of the agreement is to establish a partnership where the affiliate earns a referral fee for each approved credit card account that originates from their website. The affiliate is responsible for complying with the program's rules and restrictions, including not using restricted content or engaging in prohibited activities. Chase reserves the right to terminate the agreement if the affiliate violates these terms. The agreement also outlines the referral fee structure and the term of the agreement, which can be terminated by either party at any time with written notice.
termination_clause: Either Affiliate or Chase may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, by giving the other party written or e-mail notice of termination. Chase may also terminate this Agreement if Affiliate does not comply with the terms and conditions herein. Chase also reserves the right to terminate this Agreement in the event that any Restricted Content is incorporated on Affiliate’s site after acceptance of your registration form and the commencement of the term of this Agreement.

summary: The Real Estate Education Training Program Development Agreement is between T&B Seminars, Inc. and Legacy Education Alliance Holdings, Inc. to develop and operate a real estate seminar business using Tarek El Moussa's intellectual property. T&B grants LEA a license to use its intellectual property, while LEA is responsible for marketing and selling products. The agreement has a five-year term, renewable for successive five-year terms, with termination possible if certain cash sales benchmarks are not met. The parties must maintain confidentiality and protect each other's intellectual property, with potential penalties for breach of these obligations.
confidentiality_clause: The term "Confidential Information" means any and all information which is not readily ascertainable by proper means and which derives economic value, actual or potential from not being generally known and which has been the subject of efforts that are reasonable under the circumstances to maintain its secrecy.

summary: The co-branding agreement between About.com and ebix.com aims to create an independent co-branded channel for insurance information on the About Network. About.com will provide content and promotions, while ebix.com will offer insurance services. Both parties are obligated to maintain confidentiality and protect each other's intellectual property. Notable risks include potential liability for breaches of confidentiality, with penalties for non-compliance, although specific liability caps and termination penalties are not explicitly stated in the provided excerpt. The agreement outlines definitions for various terms, including advertising impressions and placements, to govern the partnership.

summary: The Marketing Affiliate Agreement between Birch First Global Investments Inc. and Mount Knowledge Holdings Inc. aims to formalize their prior verbal understandings regarding the marketing and support of certain technology. Mount Knowledge Holdings Inc. is granted the right to advertise, market, and sell the technology to clients, while using its best efforts to do so. The company is responsible for providing customer and technical support, protecting copyrights and trademarks, and complying with laws. The agreement can be terminated according to its cancellation provisions, with no specific liability caps or termination penalties mentioned. Both parties acknowledge that this agreement is not a franchise under applicable laws.
liability_clause: liability limitations and disclaimers contained in such materials

summary: The purpose of this collaborative research, development, and commercialization agreement is for Revolution Medicines and Sanofi to work together on the research, development, and potential commercialization of drug candidates that inhibit the activity of a specific cancer target. Revolution Medicines will grant Sanofi certain licenses, and in return, Sanofi will provide funding and support. Each party has obligations to contribute to the collaboration, and there are risks and penalties associated with non-compliance, although specific details on liability caps and termination penalties are not provided in the given excerpt. The agreement outlines definitions and terms to govern the partnership.

This co-branding agreement between VerticalNet and Neoforma aims to establish a partnership for co-branded career and training centers. VerticalNet and Neoforma agree to work together, sharing resources and expertise, with VerticalNet providing career and training content and Neoforma providing its platform and services. The parties agree to protect each other's confidential information and intellectual property. The agreement outlines the terms of their partnership, including revenue sharing and deductions, with no explicit liability caps or termination penalties mentioned in the provided excerpt. The agreement defines various terms and concepts, such as advertising, affiliates, and laboratory products, to facilitate their cooperation.

summary: The purpose of this co-branding agreement is to allow mPhase Technologies to use Lucent Technologies' name, logo, and slogan on their products and marketing materials. mPhase must abide by Lucent's guidelines and maintain the quality of their goods. Lucent has the right to terminate the agreement if mPhase's use of the logo adversely affects Lucent's rights or if mPhase's quality standards decline. Either party can terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice. Notably, neither party is liable for special, incidental, or consequential damages, and mPhase cannot assign the agreement without Lucent's consent.
termination_clause: Either party wishing to terminate the Agreement must give written notice to the other party at least thirty (30) days prior to the desired date of termination.
liability_clause: Neither Party shall be liable to the other for special, incidental, or consequential damages, even if such Party has been advised of the possibility of such damages.

summary: The Co-Branding Agreement between VerticalNet and LeadersOnline aims to facilitate the advertising of LeadersOnline's job placement services on VerticalNet's online communities. VerticalNet will permit LeadersOnline to advertise through banner ads, newsletters, and employer spotlights, and provide access to its proprietary resume bank. In return, LeadersOnline will create a link from its site to VerticalNet's site, enabling users to access VerticalNet's online communities. The agreement outlines key obligations, including the protection of confidential information and intellectual property rights. Notable risks include potential liability for breaches of confidentiality and termination penalties if either party fails to fulfill its obligations.

The co-branding agreement between Women.com and eDiets aims to create a custom co-branded center on Women.com's site, featuring eDiets' content. Women.com will host the gateway page, while eDiets will maintain the content beneath it. eDiets grants Women.com a non-exclusive license to use its content, and must deliver initial content within 15 days. Women.com reserves the right to reject content that doesn't meet its editorial guidelines. Each party bears its own expenses, and eDiets indemnifies Women.com against claims arising from inaccurate facts in its content.

The purpose of this agreement is for iMine Corporation to appoint SUNWAI Technology as a non-exclusive distributor of its cryptocurrency mining rig products in Taiwan. The distributor is responsible for marketing and selling the products, providing reports, and maintaining an inventory of products. The distributor must also comply with all applicable laws and regulations in Taiwan and not sell products outside of the territory. JRVS retains the right to designate certain customers as "House Accounts" and may compensate the distributor for sales efforts prior to such designation. The agreement does not specify notable risks or penalties, but material breaches, such as selling competitive products, may occur.
confidentiality_clause: “Confidential Information” of a party shall mean any information disclosed by that party to the other party pursuant to this Agreement which is in written, graphic, machine readable or other tangible form. Confidential Information may also include oral information disclosed by one party to the other pursuant to this Agreement.

summary: The co-branding and services agreement between RSL COM PrimeCall, Inc. and deltathree.com, Inc. aims to develop and market a co-branded prepaid IP telephony calling card. PrimeCall will provide services such as printing, toll-free access, pricing, and sales, while DeltaThree will develop a website for PrimeCall to sell its calling cards online. Both parties will jointly market the calling card and share revenues. The agreement has a term of three years, with PrimeCall having the option to terminate after 30 days' notice if certain conditions are met. DeltaThree is responsible for reimbursing PrimeCall for costs incurred, and disputes will be resolved through good faith negotiations.
termination_clause: Section 6.01 [Termination for Cause]. Termination for Cause. In the event that either PrimeCall or DeltaThree materially breaches any of its duties or obligations hereunder, which breach shall not be cured within thirty (30) days after written notice is given to the breaching party specifying the breach, then either PrimeCall or DeltaThree, as the case may be, may, by giving written notice thereof to the other, terminate this Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination, which date shall be no earlier than ten (10) days after the date of such notice. Section 6.02 [Termination for Bankruptcy]. Termination for Bankruptcy. In the event of the Bankruptcy (as hereinafter defined) of either PrimeCall or DeltaThree, then the non-bankrupt party may, by written notice thereof to the party in Bankruptcy, terminate this Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination, which date shall be no earlier than ten (10) days after the date of such notice.

summary: The purpose of this agreement is for Seattle Software Developers to develop additional features for the Pelican Delivers software, including POS integration and a customer-facing web application. The developer is obligated to complete the project within six months, with payment schedules tied to milestones. The client must pay a total of $279,000, with 20% deposits at the start and upon completion of each milestone. Notable risks include unforeseen circumstances that may change the project estimate, and the client's obligation to pay the full amount regardless of any changes.

The Consulting and Product Development Agreement is between Emerald Health Sciences Inc., Emerald Health Nutraceuticals Inc., and Michael T. Murray. The purpose is for Dr. Murray to provide consulting services for product development and validation. EHS and EHN will bear all costs associated with product development and sales, while Dr. Murray will provide guidance and services. Dr. Murray will receive monthly compensation, options to purchase EHS common stock, and royalties on net sales of products developed. The agreement has a term of ten years, with automatic renewal of the services term every two years, and can be terminated with 60 days' written notice, with EHS or EHN having the right to immediately terminate if Dr. Murray breaches the agreement.

The co-branding and advertising agreement between i-Escrow and 2TheMart aims to promote i-Escrow's escrow services to 2TheMart's auction users. i-Escrow will develop a co-branded site and information transfer mechanism, while 2TheMart will promote the services to its users. Each party is responsible for their own development and hosting costs. If the launch date is not met within four months, either party can terminate the agreement. i-Escrow will pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on transaction inquiries, with a per inquiry amount calculated as a percentage of the average transaction amount. Failure to launch or terminate the agreement may result in termination penalties.
termination_clause: In the event that the parties are unable to agree to an SOW within 2 months following the Effective Date, either party may, in its sole discretion, terminate this Agreement by providing written notice. ... In the event that, after using such efforts, the Launch Date has not occurred within 4 months following the Effective Date, either party may terminate this Agreement by providing written notice.

summary: The Development and Option Agreement between Harpoon Therapeutics, Inc. and AbbVie Biotechnology Ltd aims to develop and commercialize licensed products in a specified territory. Harpoon grants an option to AbbVie to license intellectual property rights, while AbbVie is obligated to develop and commercialize the products. Both parties have representation and warranty obligations, and they agree to indemnify each other against certain losses. The agreement includes termination provisions, with penalties for material breach, and establishes a joint governance committee to oversee collaboration. Notable risks include termination penalties and liability for breaches of warranty or intellectual property infringement.

summary: This agreement is a license, development, and commercialization agreement between Xencor and Aimmune Therapeutics. Xencor grants Aimmune a license to develop and commercialize a specific antibody, and Aimmune is obligated to use the licensed intellectual property to develop, manufacture, and commercialize the product. The parties agree to comply with applicable laws and regulations, and Aimmune is responsible for paying royalties and other payments to Xencor. The agreement includes provisions for termination and indemnification, with each party liable for its own damages, and Aimmune is subject to termination penalties if it fails to meet its obligations.

The purpose of the Manifest System Services and Co-Branding Agreement is for iShip.com to provide an internet-based shipping system to Mail Boxes Etc. USA, Inc. (MBE) for use in manifesting and shipping customer packages. iShip.com will provide the service, while MBE will utilize the system in its retail centers. Each party has obligations to maintain confidentiality and adhere to brand guidelines. Notable risks include potential liability for breach of confidentiality or intellectual property rights, and termination penalties may apply if either party fails to meet their obligations. The agreement also includes provisions for co-branding activities and the issuance of a warrant to MBE to purchase iShip.com stock.

This agreement is between Document Security Systems and HotApp International for the development of an Android software application. DSS grants a non-exclusive license to HotApp to use its technology for development purposes. HotApp will receive $23,000 per month for its services. The agreement has a 12-month term with automatic renewal unless terminated with 30 days' notice. Either party can terminate immediately if the other breaches intellectual property rights or files for bankruptcy. Upon termination, HotApp must cease use of DSS's technology and return all proprietary files. DSS retains ownership of its technology and intellectual property, and HotApp assigns any acquired rights to DSS.
termination_clause: 2.2.1. Either party may terminate this Agreement prior to expiration of the Term: (i) upon thirty (30) days prior written notice, or (ii) immediately upon written notice to the other party if: (a) the other party declares or a petition is filed in any court for insolvency or bankruptcy and such petition is not dismissed in thirty (30) days; (b) the other party reorganizes under the relevant bankruptcy act or any similar statute in such party’s jurisdiction of incorporation; (c) the other party consents to the appointment of a trustee in bankruptcy or a receiver or similar entity; or (d) the Developer breaches DSS’s Technology or Intellectual Property rights contained herein.

summary: The purpose of this co-branding agreement is for NETTAXI and SpinRecords.com to co-brand their services. NETTAXI and SpinRecords.com are obligated to brand each other's pages with their respective brand features and use reasonable commercial efforts to market each other's brand. The agreement includes licenses for the use of each other's brand features and establishes ownership of intellectual property rights. Notable risks include the potential for artificially increasing impressions on web pages, which is prohibited, and the possibility of termination if either party fails to meet their obligations.

This agreement is between NLS-1 Pharma AG and Eurofarma Laboratórios S.A. for the development and commercialization of Nolazol in Latin America. NLS grants Eurofarma an exclusive license to register, import, and sell the product, while Eurofarma is responsible for further development and commercialization. Both parties must cooperate and share information, with NLS providing know-how and Eurofarma handling regulatory obligations. The agreement includes provisions for liability, indemnities, and termination, with potential penalties for material breach or change of control. Either party can terminate the agreement with notice, and upon expiration or termination, Eurofarma's rights to the product cease.

summary: The purpose of this agreement is for MediaLinx and HealthCentral.com to co-brand a health section on the Sympatico web site. MediaLinx will host and moderate discussion forums, promote the co-branded site, and grant HealthCentral.com non-exclusive rights to use its content and marks. HealthCentral.com will integrate its content into the co-branded site, update its look and feel, and host and manage the site. Both parties are responsible for ensuring compliance with applicable laws and regulations. If user traffic falls below certain levels, MediaLinx will increase promotion efforts. The agreement outlines specific obligations and responsibilities for each party, with a focus on maintaining Canadian content and context on the co-branded site.

summary: The agreement between ARCA U.S. TREASURY FUND and ARCA CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC aims to provide blockchain administration and development services. The Blockchain Administrator will develop and administer ERC-1404 compatible digital securities and maintain smart contracts, while the Fund will pay a fee of 0.20% of its average annual net assets. The Blockchain Administrator will act as an independent contractor and maintain records in accordance with the Investment Company Act. The agreement emphasizes confidentiality and does not specify notable risks or penalties, but the Fund bears all costs and expenses not assumed by the Adviser, indicating potential financial liability.
termination_clause: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, upon 60 days' written notice, by the vote of a majority of the outstanding voting securities of the Fund or by the vote of the Fund's Trustees or by the Blockchain Administrator.
confidentiality_clause: The parties hereto agree that each shall treat confidentially the terms and conditions of this Agreement and all information provided by each party to the other regarding its business and operations. All confidential information provided by a party hereto, including nonpublic personal information (regulated pursuant to Regulation S-P), shall be used by any other party hereto solely for the purpose of rendering services pursuant to this Agreement and, except as may be required in carrying out this Agreement, shall not be disclosed to any third party, without the prior consent of such providing party.
liability_clause: The Blockchain Administrator (and its officers, managers, partners, agents, employees, controlling persons, members and any other person or entity affiliated with the Blockchain Administrator) shall not be liable to the Fund for any action taken or omitted to be taken by the Blockchain Administrator in connection with the performance of any of its duties or obligations under this Agreement or otherwise as blockchain administrator of the Fund

summary: This Business Affiliate Agreement between Dr. George D. Green and UNION DENTAL CORP. aims to facilitate the exchange of confidential information for managing dental offices. Both parties must use and disclose confidential information only as permitted by the agreement and applicable laws. They must also report any unauthorized use or disclosure and implement safeguards to maintain security. The agreement can be terminated with 30-day notice, and upon termination, all confidential information must be returned or destroyed. The parties agree to indemnify each other against losses arising from unauthorized use or disclosure of confidential information. UDC may immediately terminate this Agreement without notice and any related agreement if it determines that the Business Affiliate has breached a material provision of this Agreement

summary: The purpose of the Recipe Development Agreement is for Reed's, Inc. and Full Sail Brewing Company to collaborate on developing formulas for ginger-based flavored alcohol beverage products. Reed's will provide input, while Full Sail will develop the recipes and manufacture the products. Full Sail is responsible for development costs, except for those related to Reed's personnel. The companies will form a Development Committee to oversee the project and approve milestones. Each party represents and warrants that they have the necessary authority and approvals to enter into the agreement, and that their intellectual property does not infringe on others.

The purpose of this second amendment to the co-branding agreement is to modify the provisions of the original agreement between PC Quote, Inc. and A.B. Watley, Inc. The key obligations of each party are not explicitly stated in the amendment, but it is implied that they will continue to uphold the terms of the original agreement with the modifications made. Notable risks or penalties are not specified, but the amendment does state that its provisions will prevail in case of conflicts with the original agreement, and the parties have ratified and confirmed the agreement with the modifications.

The marketing affiliate agreement between Equidata and National Credit Report.com allows the Marketing Affiliate to market Equidata's personal credit services. The Marketing Affiliate is responsible for collecting payments from consumers and paying Equidata according to the agreed-upon compensation schedule. Equidata reserves the right to increase service costs with 30 days' notice. The agreement includes provisions for dispute resolution through arbitration and imposes penalties for late payments, including a 1.5% monthly late charge and 25% attorney's fees. The Marketing Affiliate is also prohibited from soliciting Equidata's existing clients and must comply with relevant laws and regulations, including the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

The purpose of this agreement is to amend the existing Distributor Agreement between Zebra and ScanSource to include Xplore products. Zebra is obligated to sell Xplore products to ScanSource, while ScanSource is obligated to purchase and resell these products to members of the Zebra PartnerConnect Program. Notable risks include the requirement for Zebra to obtain regulatory approvals for the sale of Xplore products, and the application of different warranty terms for Xplore products. The agreement can be terminated at any time by either party in accordance with the termination provisions of the Distributor Agreement.

contract_id: ClickstreamCorp_20200330_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_12089935_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Development Agreement
summary: The Application Development Agreement is between InfinixSoft Global LLC and Clickstream Corporation, where InfinixSoft will design an application for Clickstream. InfinixSoft must create the application as detailed in Exhibit A, provide services in a workmanlike manner, and deliver the application system to Clickstream. Clickstream must provide assistance and cooperation, initial information, and content for the application. The agreement can be terminated by either party with a 7-day notice, or due to a material breach or force majeure event. Upon termination, Clickstream must pay InfinixSoft for services rendered, and InfinixSoft must deliver all intellectual property to Clickstream.
termination_clause: This Agreement may be terminated: 1. By either Party on provision of seven (7) days written notice to the other Party in case of a Force Majeure Event. 2. Client has the unilateral right to cancel this agreement at any time within a 7-day notice period. Further, Developer can only cancel due to lack of payment. Client will have a 30 day right to cure before a cancelation can occur. 3. By either Party for a material breach of any provision of this Agreement by the other Party, if the other Party’s material breach is not cured within three (3) days of receipt of written notice thereof. 4. By the Client at any time and without prior notice, if the Developer is convicted of any crime or offense, fails or refuses to comply with the written policies or reasonable directives of the Client, or is guilty of serious misconduct in connection with performance under this Agreement.
confidentiality_clause: The Developer agrees, during the Term and thereafter, to hold in strictest confidence, and not to use, except for the benefit of the Client, or to disclose to any person, firm, or corporation without the prior written authorization of the Client, any Confidential Information of the Client.

summary: The purpose of this software development agreement is for OBA Seattle Software Developers to design, develop, and deliver the "Pelican Delivers Application Phase 1" to Pelican Delivers Inc. The developer is obligated to provide services and deliverables according to the project specifications, while the client must provide necessary information and conduct acceptance tests in good faith. Notable risks include the client's option to terminate the agreement if the developer fails to correct defects or meet acceptance criteria, and the developer's obligation to continue performing services despite acceptance or rejection of milestone deliverables.
termination_clause: Should Developer reasonably fail to meet the requirements of Section 3. or Section 3.5 of this Agreement such that a Milestone Deliverable or Modified Deliverable does not satisfy Client's reasonable acceptance criteria within the time periods set forth in the same, Client will have the option of: (a) repeating the procedures set forth in Section 3.4 or Section 3.5 Above; or (b) terminating this Agreement pursuant to Section 8 of this agreement.
confidentiality_clause: 6.4.2 Non-Disclosure. Neither Party shall use or disclose Confidential Information of the other Party to any third party, without the written consent of the Disclosing Party of such Confidential Information.

summary: The Business Development Agreement between Liquidmetal Technologies, Inc. and Eutectix, LLC aims to collaborate on the development and commercialization of amorphous alloy materials and technologies. Liquidmetal grants Eutectix a license to use its equipment and alloys, while Eutectix will provide product development and manufacturing services. The agreement has a five-year term, with automatic extensions unless terminated with 180 days' notice. Notably, the licensed equipment and alloys are provided "as is" with no warranties, and Eutectix bears all delivery and shipping expenses. The agreement also includes royalty-bearing licenses to Liquidmetal's patents and technical information, with restrictions on the field of use.
termination_clause: The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue until the fifth (5 th) anniversary of the Effective Date (the “Term”), unless the Agreement is sooner terminated in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Upon the expiration of the Term, the Term shall automatically extend for successive 12-month periods until one Party terminates the Agreement by providing at least 180 days prior written notice to the other Party prior to the expiration of the then-current term or unless sooner terminated in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.

summary: The El Pollo Loco Franchise Development Agreement grants a developer the right to establish and operate El Pollo Loco restaurants within a specified territory. The developer is obligated to comply with the terms of the agreement and develop restaurants according to a market development plan. El Pollo Loco retains the right to establish and operate restaurants outside of the territory and sell similar products through other channels. The agreement includes provisions for termination and indemnification, with the developer liable for any damages or losses incurred by El Pollo Loco due to the developer's breach of the agreement.
liability_clause: Franchisor shall have no liability under any lease or purchase agreement for any El Pollo Loco ® Restaurant location developed under this Agreement and shall not guarantee Developer’s obligations thereunder.

summary: The purpose of this co-branding agreement is for Snap Technologies and United Airlines to collaborate on creating co-branded web pages. Snap will develop and host these pages, while United will supply content and brand features. Both parties grant each other non-exclusive licenses to use their respective marks and content. The agreement requires compliance with each party's content and technical guidelines. Notable risks include potential liability for misuse of trademarks or confidential information, and termination penalties if either party fails to fulfill their obligations. The agreement aims to promote each other's services and products, with both parties retaining ownership of their intellectual property rights.

The co-branding agreement between PC Quote, Inc. and AB Wately, Inc. allows ABW to offer PCQ's software and market data feed on its website. PCQ will provide the software and feed, while ABW will sign up new subscribers and ensure they agree to the terms. ABW must include disclaimers on its pages and not reproduce or redistribute the service. The agreement has a monthly payment structure, with a finance charge for late payments. Either party can terminate the agreement if the other is in material breach, with PCQ having the right to terminate access to the service if payments are not made.
termination_clause: Neither PCQ nor ABW shall terminate or alter this Agreement except as stated herein. ... should a party to this Agreement be in material breach of the Agreement, the other party may terminate the Agreement thirty (30) days after notice of said material breach is received, and only if such material breach is not cured within thirty (30) days of receipt of notice. ... In the event that any invoice is not paid by ABW within forty-five (45) days after receipt, and when no discrepancy issues have been identified by ABW which are in some stage of resolution, after giving notice to ABW, PCQ may terminate this agreement
confidentiality_clause: 8. Confidentiality of Proprietary Information A. ABW understands and acknowledges the proprietary nature of the HYPERFEED and SOFTWARE provided by PCQ and that said HYPERFEED and SOFTWARE have been developed as a trade secret of PCQ and at its expense. ABW agrees to hold said information in the same manner as ABW deals with its own proprietary information and trade secrets. Furthermore, ABW agrees not to attempt any reverse engineering of the HYPERFEED to decode the signals used by PCQ in transmitting the information. B. PCQ understands the proprietary nature of any information belonging to ABW, and recognizes the harm that can be occasioned to user by disclosure of information relative to ABW's activities, PCQ agrees to hold such information in the same manner as PCQ deals with its own proprietary information and trade secrets. C. PCQ acknowledges the confidential nature of ABW's use of the SOFTWARE and HYPERFEED during the initial term of this agreement.
liability_clause: LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT FROM ANY AND ALL CAUSES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PROGRAM MALFUNCTION OR OPERATIONAL NEGLIGENCE, SHALL BE LIMITED TO GENERAL MONEY DAMAGES IN AN AMOUNT NOT TO EXCEED THE TOTAL CHARGES PAID BY ABW FOR THE SERVICES DURING THE MOST RECENT TWELVE (12) MONTHS OF THE AGREEMENT.

summary: This agreement is an exclusive license and product development contract between Eton Pharmaceuticals and Aucta Pharmaceuticals. Eton obtains an exclusive license to develop and market Aucta's pharmaceutical products in a specified territory. In return, Eton will pay Aucta milestone, royalty, and licensing payments based on sales. Both parties have obligations to comply with applicable laws and regulations. The agreement includes provisions for indemnification and liability, with each party responsible for losses incurred due to their own breach or negligence. Termination penalties and liability caps are also outlined, although specific details are omitted for competitive reasons.

This agreement is between Co-Diagnostics, Inc. and PreCheck Health Services, Inc. for the distribution of Co-Diagnostics' products, including qPCR infectious disease kits and diagnostic tests, in Romania. The distributor agrees to promote and sell the products, while the principal maintains the right to contact and market to potential customers. The agreement can be terminated by either party with 30 days' written notice, and the distributor is entitled to receive and sell any orders accepted prior to termination. The distributor is an independent contractor, and the agreement is governed by Texas law, with no assignment allowed without the principal's written consent.
termination_clause: This Agreement shall be in effect until March 18. 2021, unless sooner terminated by either party upon (30) days written notice, without cause.

contract_id: GentechHoldingsInc_20190808_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11776814_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Distributor Agreement
summary: The purpose of this agreement is for B & C General Warehouse Corporation LLC to appoint XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX as its exclusive distributor of coffee products in the USA. The distributor is obligated to purchase products from the company for resale, while the company is obligated to sell the products to the distributor at agreed-upon prices. The distributor must limit its activities to the designated territory and refrain from selling products outside of it. Notable risks include the distributor's responsibility for freight and shipping costs, as well as its obligation to hold the company harmless in case of any liability resulting from the resale of products.
confidentiality_clause: Unless otherwise expressly authorized by the Disclosing Party, the Recipient Party agrees that it and any of its personnel receiving Proprietary Information under this Agreement shall treat such Proprietary Information in strict confidence with the same degree of care applied to its own Proprietary Information of like importance, which it does not wish to disclose, publish, or disseminate to third parties.
liability_clause: Company shall have no control over or any liability in connection with the price at which Distributor resells the Products, and Distributor shall hold Company harmless and indemnify and defend Company from and against any liability resulting therefrom.
